title: senecio subumbellatus var. subumbellatus
intro: Senecio subumbellatus var. subumbellatus, also known as the small-flowered ragwort, is a variety of the Senecio subumbellatus plant, a flowering plant in the daisy family. It is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and alkaloid compounds, which have been reported to exhibit antimicrobial, antifungal, and antioxidant activities. In herbal preparations, Senecio subumbellatus var. subumbellatus is often used as a tea, tincture, or infusion to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory symptoms.
